# ICICI — Usage Guide

*Mode: agent · 🤖 needs an LLM endpoint*

Converts ICICI net-banking statement downloads (the .xls files) into a GnuCash-importable CSV in canonical columns.

## When to use it

Use this for the .xls statements ICICI lets you download from net-banking. To also map rows to GnuCash accounts in one step, use GnuCash Import and choose ICICI instead.

## Inputs

- **ICICI statement XLS file(s)** (required) — accepts: Excel 97-2003 (.xls) — one or many.
  - One or more ICICI .xls statement downloads.
  - ⚠️ Use the original .xls export from ICICI net-banking. If you re-saved it as .xlsx, export a fresh .xls — the layout this skill expects is the net-banking .xls.

## How to run

1. Download your statement(s) as .xls from ICICI net-banking.
2. Select the file(s).
3. Click Run and download the CSV.

## Output

Output is written to the **`Data/outputs/`** folder, named:

```
Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-ICICI_GnuCash.csv
```

Files produced:

- **`YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-ICICI_GnuCash.csv`** — The statement in canonical date / description / amount / balance columns — the same shape every bank skill emits, ready for the GnuCash account-mapping pipeline.

## Tips

Selecting several months at once produces one combined CSV in statement order.

## Troubleshooting

| If… | Then… |
|------|-------|
| Run errors mentioning the endpoint / model. | ICICI currently uses an LLM step. Start Ollama or set an endpoint in Settings. |
| Parsing fails or columns are off. | Confirm it is the genuine net-banking .xls, not a converted/edited copy. |
